creator: rmgls@free.fr
text:
On, 20 July 2012
Carlo Strub <cs@FreeBSD.org>,freebsd-ports-bugs@FreeBSD.org,bug-followup@Fr wrote:

> > > Synopsis: audio/csound: csound5.17XX requires portaudio 19

>> > State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
>> > State-Changed-By: cs
>> > State-Changed-When: Fri Jul 20 19:44:13 UTC 2012
>> > State-Changed-Why:=20

> > Could it be that it works with portaudio2 port? Please try and tell us.

> > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=3D169541


>> Hi,
>> yes, you are right, it compiles with portaudio2 (v19.20071207j).
>> But csound 5.17.11 crashes here just after writing the message:
>> creating /tmp/XXX.sco.


Hi, Carlo,

The Csound list helped me to solve the problem.

in fact, there are important changes since two versions of Csound.
the old libraries and binary files seated in /usr/X11R6 => bin and libs 
were not deleted properly, by make deinstall, so there was a version mismatch.
you can easily see this.
deleting all csound files seated in /usr/X11R6 made the job.
Csound v5.17.11 works fine now on current.

with this correction if needed you can close the PR.

Thanks, and best regards.

Raoul
rmgls@free.fr
